business.headline: US delegation to visit India from June 1–4 to finalise interim trade pactThe discussions are expected to focus on important areas such as market access, non-tariff measures, customs and trade facilitation, investment promotion and economic security alignment.

Sensex slips 142 points, Nifty ends flat as auto and metal stocks support marketThe S&P BSE Sensex fell 141.90 points, or 0.19%, to close at 75,867.80. Meanwhile, the NSE Nifty50 slipped just 6.55 points, or 0.03%, to settle at 23,907.15.

Air India to cut domestic flights by 22% as Iran war pushes fuel costs higherThe cuts will affect Air India's domestic operations for June and July, while decisions for August are yet to be finalised.

HDFC Bank responds to Rs 45 crore payment allegations, denies 'wrongdoing'HDFC Bank shares fell sharply during Wednesday's trading session. The stock dropped nearly 2.6%, trading at Rs 758.75 on the NSE around 1:29 pm, down Rs 20.15.

HDFC Bank shares drop 2% on report of probe into Rs 45-crore interest paymentsThe stock dropped nearly 2.6% during the trading session after the report surfaced. As per NSE data cited in the report, HDFC Bank shares were trading at Rs 758.75, down Rs 20.15 or 2.59%, around 1:29 pm.

8th Pay Commission: Why a union wants 5 different fitment factors instead of oneThe Indian Railway Technical Supervisors' Association (IRTSA) has urged the 8th Pay Commission to replace a single fitment factor with five graded multipliers. The demand shifts the discussion from overall salary hikes to how different levels should be paid.

Air India trims domestic flights by 15–20% during July–August as fuel costs soarIn a statement, an Air India spokesperson said the airline has temporarily adjusted some domestic services after earlier changes to international routes.

How AI helped Taiwan overtake India to become the world's 5th largest stock marketTaiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company is the world's largest contract chipmaker and plays a major role in producing advanced semiconductors used in AI systems.

Not a finding of fraud, dishonesty: Byju Raveendran on jail sentence in SingaporeResponding to the developments, Raveendran said the matter has been misunderstood and is part of ongoing settlement talks with lenders and stakeholders.

From Messi to mounting lawsuits: Inside the collapse of Byju's empireA Singapore court sentenced Byju Raveendran to six months in jail for contempt over asset-related orders. The ruling sharpens focus on Byju's steep fall from a $22 billion edtech darling to a company facing debt, lawsuits and insolvency proceedings.

Air India, BigBasket, Tata Electronics: Inside Tata's key boardroom discussionsTata Sons' latest board meeting stayed calm as operating chiefs answered questions on Air India, BigBasket and other businesses. The discussions showed tighter scrutiny of profits, capital allocation and the group's long-term strategic bets.

Sensex, Nifty open flat as Middle East tensions keep investors cautiousThe S&P BSE Sensex slipped 22.10 points, or 0.03%, to 75,987.60 in early trade, while the NSE Nifty50 declined 6.95 points, or 0.03%, to 23,906.75.

Byju Raveendran sentenced to six months in jail by Singapore courtByju Raveendran, founder of embattled edtech company Byju's, has been sentenced to six months in jail by a Singapore court for contempt of court.

8th Pay Commission: Odisha consultations scheduled for July 6-7The 8th Pay Commission will visit Bhubaneswar on July 6 and 7 for consultations with organisations, unions and employee associations. The outreach comes as the panel widens regional talks and gathers demands on pay, allowances and pensions.

Indian economy is quite robust: Arvind PanagariyaIn an exclusive interview with India Today, Arvind Panagariya, Chairman of the sixteenth Finance Commission, asserts that the domestic economy remains fundamentally robust, citing a steady gross foreign direct investment of ninety-four billion dollars for the recent fiscal year and consistent growth averaging over seven percent in the past three years.

When centralised manufacturing, record irregularities conveniently escaped detectionIn the eighth part of this series on labour laws and Inspector Raj, this article examines how seemingly separate establishments functioned as one operational unit, while material irregularities in records and manufacturing activity went unnoticed.

Hidden cost of AI adoption: Why enterprises are worried about token economicsEnterprises are reassessing AI roll-outs as token and compute costs climb with wider internal use. The shift is pushing companies to focus on cost control, ROI and sustainable scale.

When it rains, it pours: Why Nithin Kamath thinks 2026 could be tough for householdsIn a post on X, Kamath flagged concerns around a possible super El Nino, weak rainfall and rising energy costs linked to global tensions. According to him, if food and fuel prices rise together, inflation could become a much bigger challenge for Indian households.

ITR filing this year? 5 changes that make it different from last yearPlanning to file your ITR soon? Before you begin, it may be worth slowing down for a moment. From new disclosure requirements to tweaks in reporting rules, tax return filing this year comes with a few important changes that many taxpayers may easily miss. Here are five key updates to know.

Markets end lower as renewed Iran tensions push rupee downThe S&P BSE Sensex fell 479.26 points, or 0.63%, to close at 76,009.70, while the NSE Nifty50 declined 118 points, or 0.49%, to settle at 23,913.70.

entertainment.headline: I asked God for death: Honey Singh opens up on bipolar disorder struggle Rapper-singer Honey Singh recalled asking God for death in 2019 during his battle with bipolar disorder. He said that night, along with treatment and reflection, changed his life and gave him a reason to live.

Priya Patidar on Welcome to the Jungle: Surreal to sing track for Raveena Tandon Priya Patidar said recording the Welcome to the Jungle title track and singing for Raveena Tandon felt surreal. She said the release marked her long-awaited playback breakthrough and carried emotional weight for her family.

Vijay Deverakonda-Rashmika Mandanna airport appearance sparks buzz over his pendant Actors Vijay Deverakonda and Rashmika Mandanna were seen together at the airport as a video of them surfaced online. Fans quickly focused on Vijay's 'R' pendant, linking it to Rashmika ahead of their film reunion in Ranabaali.

Jackie Shroff reacts to son Tiger's box office failures, says not in our hands Jackie Shroff has spoken about son Tiger Shroff's recent box office struggles, saying success and failure are not in an actor's hands. He added that Tiger remains focused on working sincerely and leaving the rest to the audience.

Marriage never changes boundaries: Sonakshi Sinha on filming intimate scenes In an exclusive conversation, actors Sonakshi Sinha and Jyotika opened up about intimacy on screen, personal boundaries, marriage, and working with Ashwiny Iyer Tiwari in System.

Bengali filmmaker Anik Dutta dies at 62 after falling from terrace in Kolkata Filmmaker Anik Dutta died after allegedly falling from a terrace in Kolkata's Hindustan Park area on Wednesday. Police are probing the circumstances, with a suicide note reportedly recovered and his post-mortem pending.

Bhumi Pednekar reacts to alleged assault of 9-year-old girl in UP: Broken society Bhumi Pednekar reacted with shock after a nine-year-old girl was allegedly sexually assaulted by two minor boys in Uttar Pradesh's Ballia district. Calling it a sign of a "broken society", the actor questioned the environment children are growing up in and urged people to take the issue seriously.

Actors body chief Poonam Dhillon says Ranveer 'could have come to us' amid Don 3 row Poonam Dhillon expressed disappointment that Cine and TV Artistes' Association (CINTAA) was not approached in the Ranveer Singh-Don 3 dispute. The association says it cannot take a stand until it knows the full facts.

Maa Hai Na trailer: Shilpa Shetty's show promises kitchen chaos and laughter The trailer of Maa Hai Na introduces popular television personalities cooking with their mothers. The upcoming ZEE5 series, hosted by Shilpa Shetty Kundra, turns kitchen challenges into a test of family bonds.

Bobby Deol had one rule before Aashram release: tell wife about intimate scenes Bobby Deol has revealed that he first told his wife Tanya about Prakash Jha's Aashram series after hearing the story and its intimate scenes.

Kattalan makers ask viewers to stay till end for three post-credit scenes The makers of Malayalam film Kattalan have asked audiences to remain seated after the film ends. The advisory points to three post-credit scenes and sharpens interest in the thriller's final reveal.

What CM Vijay's new cinema rule means after fan's death, early shows ban in state Chief Minister Joseph Vijay has amended Tamil Nadu cinema rules to allow theatres five shows a day for the first week of new Tamil releases without separate approval. The move brings relief to producers and exhibitors, but the ban on early morning screenings remains unchanged.

No remix can out-swag OG Chunnari Chunnari. That is peak Salman Khan Bollywood keeps remixing songs, but nobody is remixing the magic Salman Khan and Sushmita Sen brought to OG Chunnari Chunnari. Sorry, not sorry.

Imtiaz Ali has his 'eyes on' Alia Bhatt, hints at reunion after Highway Imtiaz Ali has hinted at working with Alia Bhatt again more than a decade after Highway. The remark has revived interest in the film that reshaped her career and remains a key title in his body of work.

Governor trailer: Manoj Bajpayee is RBI chief during India's 1990 financial crisis The trailer of Governor shows Manoj Bajpayee as an RBI chief navigating India's 1990 financial crisis amid rising public unrest and political pressure. The film also stars Adah Sharma as a journalist questioning the controversial decisions taken during the turbulent period.

Obsession review: Curry Barker's psychological horror is impossible to shake offRelease Date:Fri May 29, 2026 Curry Barker's Obsession follows a lonely young man whose wish for reciprocated love turns catastrophic. The film uses that premise to explore loneliness, entitlement and the horror of treating love like possession.

Sri Sri Ravi Shankar meets celebs: Vikrant Massey touches feet, Sunil Grover jokes Actors Vikrant Massey, Sunil Grover, director Rajkumar Hirani and producer Sajid Nadiadwala, among others, recently met spiritual leader Sri Sri Ravi Shankar. Several videos and photos from the interaction have now surfaced online.

Fact Check: Did Ranveer Singh meet Sadhguru amid Don 3 row? A video of Ranveer Singh questioning Sadhguru about the purpose of life is going viral on social media. While the video is original, the timing is not.

Vishal Dadlani denies Indian Idol exit after NEET paper leak criticism Vishal Dadlani dismissed rumours that he had been removed from Indian Idol after his NEET remarks. His post shifted attention to online misinformation and his criticism of the exam leak response.

general news.headline: How DK Shivakumar outplayed SiddaramaiahSiddaramaiah believed numbers and a strong support base would shield him from any leadership challenge. But DK Shivakumar hit the reset button in Karnataka with the blessings of those who really call the shots -- the Congress high command.

Tamil Nadu CM Vijay meets PM Modi in Delhi after 12 years, talks last 25 minutesTamil Nadu Chief Minister Vijay's first Delhi visit after taking office turned politically significant as he met PM Modi after 12 years, triggering fresh speculation over equations, alliances and the fast-changing power dynamics in the state.

TMC's Kakoli Dastidar quits all party posts as Suvendu Adhikari meet stirs BJP buzzThe development provided a major blow to Trinamool Congress as Dastidar was considered a close aide of party supremo Mamata Banerjee for a long time.

Head shaved, hands cuffed: Bengal cops parade arrested TMC goons in undergarmentsThe police in West Bengal have paraded at least three TMC-linked strongmen in undergarments through the streets after their arrests. Akash Singh, a don from Howrah, had his head and face shaven too. The parades come amid a police crackdown and amid the TMC's allegations of legal and moral breach.
The CIA, the T-72, and the Delhi Gymkhana Club - a Cold War taleHow the Delhi Gymkhana Club was the stage for a CIA intelligence coup involving the Indian Army's Soviet-built tank fleet.

ED team attacked in Kerala, car windows smashed after raids on Pinarayi VijayanCPI(M) workers blocked and vandalised an ED vehicle during statewide protests over raids at premises linked to Pinarayi Vijayan and his daughter in an alleged pay-off case.

Why Siddaramaiah is no pushover even for mighty Congress brassFor at least three years, the Congress high command carefully balanced Siddaramaiah's role as the Karnataka Chief Minister and his deputy DK Shivakumar's aspirations. While DKS might have his CM dream fulfilled at last, the mighty Congress brass walks a risky path in asking Siddu to step aside. Here's why.

SC asks CBSE to clarify 3-language rule for Class 9 in 4 weeks, next hearing in JulyThe Supreme Court has sought responses from CBSE and the Centre on petitions against the new three-language policy for Class 9 within four weeks. The next hearing dates are July 15–16. Petitioners say the shift, set to begin in 2026, may add pressure on students preparing for board exams.

Cyprus offers India a Mediterranean foothold amid Islamic Nato axisIndia's new partnership with Cyprus ranges from defence and maritime security to counter-terror cooperation. This could give New Delhi a foothold in the Eastern Mediterranean at a time when the Pakistan-Turkey-Saudi alignment is becoming more visible.

Each Samsung chip employee to get Rs 3.25 crore bonus after bumper profitSamsung's largest union has approved a massive bonus package for employees in the company's semiconductor division. As per the approval, every employee is eligible for as much as Rs 3.25 crore as bonus pay this year. Here are the details.

BJP MP claims 50 Trinamool MLAs, 20 MPs ready to defect as Bengal turmoil deepensBJP MP Saumitra Khan claimed in Bankura that several Trinamool legislators and MPs were ready to join his party. The remarks, along with his attack on Abhishek Banerjee, drew a sharp denial from Trinamool and deepened speculation over unrest in the party.

El Nino confirmed, alert sounded by Australia: Should India be worried?The Pacific Ocean is changing and the shift that could reshape India's weather is closer than we realise. Here's when a potentially historic El Nino could hit.

Water and kindness: Why PM's mantra to tackle soaring summer heat clicksAs parts of India are witnessing soaring temperatures, Prime Minister Narendra Modi has advised Indians to show kindness, and offer a glass of water to others. His message is not just about staying hydrated. His message, in line with India's age-old traditions, can become a lifeline for someone in this extreme heat.

AI giving no gains, tech layoffs happening for no real reason: Sridhar VembuWeeks before top AI leaders started softening their warnings around job losses, Zoho founder Sridhar Vembu was already questioning whether AI was truly delivering real productivity gains. He also suggested companies may be using AI to cut labour cost and impress investors.

Live: Fulfilled all promises to people, Siddaramaiah asserts amid resignation buzzKarnataka Leadership Buzz Live Updates: Karnataka is headed for a major political transition, with sources indicating Chief Minister Siddaramaiah is likely to resign on Thursday at 11 am following marathon meetings with the Congress high command in Delhi. Siddaramaiah has also sought an appointment with Governor Thaawarchand Gehlot tomorrow, further fuelling speculation over an imminent change of guard in the state. Deputy CM D. K. Shivakumar, widely seen as the frontrunner for the top post, has extended his stay in Delhi and is expected to attend a breakfast meeting called by Siddaramaiah at the CM's Bengaluru residence on Thursday morning. Sources said crucial discussions involving Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge, senior leader Rahul Gandhi and Karnataka leaders centred around honouring the reported power-sharing understanding in the state. Stay tuned to indiatoday.in for all the latest updates.
Can ethanol mixed in water burn as Nitin Gadkari claims? Science decodedNitin Gadkari has said a stove using ethanol mixed with water can produce cooking flames at lower cost than LPG.

Why Pomfret and Hilsa are becoming rare on Indian platesIn this week's Climate on My Plate, we explore how the cultural and culinary relevance of two of India's most popular fish is at risk as temperatures rise and warm our oceans.

Assam clears Uniform Civil Code Bill, 3rd state to do so after Uttarakhand, GujaratThe Bill, however, said that it will not be applicable to any of the Scheduled Tribes residing in Assam. It proposes several punitive measures, including seven years imprisonment for bigamy or polygamy, and three months in jail for not registering a live-in relationship.

Karnataka Chief Minister Siddaramaiah to resign tomorrow: SourcesFuelling the speculation further, sources in the Chief Minister's Office confirmed that Siddaramaiah has sought an appointment with Karnataka Governor Thaawarchand Gehlot for May 28.

SIR had become a fait accompli: Yogendra Yadav on why he skipped court hearingYogendra Yadav alleged that the SIR exercise became a "fait accompli" as the Election Commission of India continued rolling out successive phases during the Supreme Court hearing. He further said that, in his view, this effectively amounted to large-scale disenfranchisement of voters and marked a potential erosion of constitutional safeguards.

Pakistani extremists prevent Lahore from returning to original Hindu, Sikh namesWeeks after clearing the proposal, the Maryam Nawaz-led provincial government of Pakistan's Punjab has deferred the decision to restore the pre-Partition Hindu and British-era names of several Lahore landmarks. According to reports, the government was forced to make a U-turn after it faced criticism from "extremists" and social media vloggers.

Can deletion during SIR cost you citizenship? Supreme Court's big orderThe Supreme Court verdict is crucial as the petitioners argued that the SIR exercise, which is being conducted by the Election Commission, was a citizenship verification exercise by backdoor.

No remix can out-swag OG Chunnari Chunnari. That is peak Salman KhanBollywood keeps remixing songs, but nobody is remixing the magic Salman Khan and Sushmita Sen brought to OG Chunnari Chunnari. Sorry, not sorry.

India's timely gesture for Pakistan despite hostile rhetoric over Indus watersIndia has alerted Pakistan to possible flooding in the Chenab after opening the Salal Dam spillway gates in Jammu and Kashmir. The warning comes despite a freeze of the Indus Waters Treaty and Pakistan continuing to vilify India globally.

sports.headline: Manchester United back to generating profit after securing UCL football Manchester United posted a 37.7 million pound operating profit for the nine months ending March 31, 2026. Driven by cuts, compliance and a top-three finish, means that United are on track to complete a major financial turnaround.

Dhoni's CSK future rests with him, says team CEO IPL 2026: CSK CEO Kasi Viswanathan said MS Dhoni alone will decide his future with the franchise. The remarks keep open roles as player, coach or mentor as Chennai continue their transition.

SRH vs RR Live: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi set for big Eliminator test vs Hyderabad Sunrisers Hyderabad (SRH) vs Rajasthan Royals (RR) Live Score, IPL 2026 Playoffs: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi headlines a high-stakes Eliminator as RR face a dangerous SRH side. SRH enter the clash with two wins over RR already this season, but the odds remain equal in this high-value clash in Mullanpur.

Norway Chess: Gukesh, Praggnanandhaa lose as Divya continues her winning run Gukesh and Praggnanandhaa let promising Norway Chess positions drift into Armageddon defeats. Divya Deshmukh kept India in the conversation, while Firouzja and Carlsen underlined how brutally this format punishes hesitation.

Singapore Open: HS Prannoy stages brilliant fightback to dump out Jonatan Christie Singapore Open: HS Prannoy came from a game down to beat fifth seed Jonatan Christie at the Singapore Open. The comeback sent him into the Round of 16, where he will face Kidambi Srikanth or Loh Kean Yew.

Randhir Singh, India's first Asian Games shooting gold medallist, dies at 79 Indian sport mourns Randhir Singh, India's first Asian Games shooting gold medallist and veteran sports administrator, whose legacy spanned five Olympics and the global Olympic movement.

French Open, Day 4 LIVE:Swaitek advances to third round, Djokovic in action later French Open 2026, Day 4 Live updates: Iga Swiatek goes into the third round with a dominating 6-2,6-3 win over Sara Bejlek. Later today, Novak Djokovic will be on the court as he faces Valentin Royer in the second round.

SRH have Plan A, B and C ready for Vaibhav Sooryavanshi: Pat Cummins IPL 2026: Sunrisers Hyderabad captain Pat Cummins admitted that SRH have prepared multiple bowling plans for Rajasthan Royals teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi ahead of the Eliminator clash. The SRH skipper reflected on Vaibhav's stunning century against SRH earlier this season and said Hyderabad's bowlers have learned important lessons from that defeat.

Sinner's French Open pursuit hits a snag. It's got nothing to do with tennis Roland Garros: World Number One Jannik Sinner began his French Open campaign with a dominant straight-sets victory over Clément Tabur, extending his incredible winning streak to 30 consecutive matches. But his biggest challenge is not even on the clay court.

RCB used to rely on individual superstars, now they win as a unit IPL Qualifier 1, RCB vs GT: Royal Challengers Bengaluru's shift from a superstar-led model to a balanced team has come into focus through recent assessments by Ambati Rayudu and Tom Moody. The change reflects a wider structural overhaul in management and philosophy, with shared roles now driving results.

IPL 2026 Eliminator: Sooryavanshi's playoffs pressure test meets familiar SRH IPL 2026 Eliminator, SRH vs RR: Rajasthan Royals and Sunrisers Hyderabad meet in the IPL Eliminator with Vaibhav Sooryavanshi under the spotlight. The contest pits RR's teenage surge against SRH's winning record, batting depth and familiarity with knockout routes.

Aryaman Birla salutes Rajat Patidar's Qualifier 1 blitzIPL 2026: Now RCB chairman, Aryaman Birla hailed former Madhya Pradesh teammate Rajat Patidar after his explosive Qualifier 1 innings against GT. Patidar's unbeaten 93 off just 33 balls powered RCB into a second straight IPL final

Bhuvneshwar Kumar reinvents himself, hurls RCB into IPL 2026 finalRCB vs GT, IPL 2026 Final: Bhuvneshwar Kumar's spell in Qualifier 1 helped Royal Challengers Bengaluru reached the IPL 2026 final. His shift from knuckle balls to scrambled seam underlined the experience behind his Purple Cap surge.

French Open 2026 Day 4 order of play: Djokovic, Basavareddy headline a big listNovak Djokovic meets Valentin Royer and Nishesh Basavareddy faces Alex Michelsen as Day 4 opens at Roland Garros. The schedule places a title chase and a breakthrough run side by side in Paris.

India Women's pre-T20 World Cup checklist: Key areas to fix in England seriesWomen's T20 World Cup 2026: Harmanpreet Kaur's India have plenty to focus on in the series against England as they build towards their quest for a maiden world T20 title.

We are coming for you: Patidar sounds warning after RCB storm into IPL finalRCB vs GT, IPL 2026 Qualifier 1: Rajat Patidar played a sensational innings to take RCB into the final of IPL 2026. After hammering Gujarat Titans, Patidar sounded a warning to the bowlers – we are coming for you.

The Patidar Way: RCB captain, under the radar and above the restRCB vs GT, IPL 2026 Qualifier 1: In a league built on personality and spectacle, Rajat Patidar is a quiet anomaly. He says little, seeks nothing, and delivers everything. RCB's captain is unlike anyone the IPL has seen before.

Qualifier 1 Play of the Day: When Shubman Gill's gamble backfired on GujaratIPL Qualifier 1, Play of the Day: Gujarat Titans' batting line-up tanked in Dharamsala on Tuesday, as they tried chasing a record total in the IPL playoffs. But batting failure was not why the team lost, nor was their bowling performance in the last 5 overs, where they gave away 104 runs.

Virat Kohli, de Villiers share nostalgic hug after RCB charge into finals of IPLRCB thrashed Gujarat Titans by 92 runs to reach a second straight IPL final, with Virat Kohli embracing AB de Villiers after the finish. The result kept their title defence alive and turned an old partnership into the night's enduring image.

Zee Entertainment in talks with FIFA over World Cup broadcast rights in IndiaIndia's Zee Entertainment Enterprises is in talks with FIFA to secure the streaming and broadcast rights for the 2026 FIFA World Cup in India, the company said on Tuesday, a move that could shake up the country's sports broadcasting landscape.

I feel like the Eiffel Tower: Naomi Osaka revels in dramatic French Open fashionJapan's former world number one Naomi Osaka said she is beginning to embrace her role as an entertainer on the WTA Tour after turning heads with a dazzling golden outfit during her first-round win at the French Open on Tuesday.

Ee Salavu Cup Namde? Ruthless RCB demolish GT to book direct ticket to finalRCB vs GT, IPL Qualifier 1: Inevitable? Rajat Patidar's 33-ball 93 anchored a record 254, before RCB's bowlers decimated Gujarat Titans with ease. In Dharamsala on Tuesday, Royal Challengers Bengaluru booked their place in a second consecutive IPL final, one win away from history.

Sriram Balaji, Yuki Bhambri make winning start to French Open in men's doublesIndia's Yuki Bhambri and N Sriram Balaji moved into the second round of the men's doubles event at the French Open 2026 after winning their respective opening-round matches in Paris on Tuesday.

French teenager becomes youngest male Grand Slam match-winner in 17 yearsWildcard teenager Moise Kouame stunned former champion Marin Cilic on his Grand Slam debut at Roland Garros. The straight-sets win underlined a rapid rise and delivered a rare age-record breakthrough.

A 3-hour ad film? Viral video takes cheeky dig at IPL's commercial overdriveIPL 2026: Popular X personality Ramesh Srivats takes a cheeky swipe at the relentless advertising creeping into modern cricket broadcasts, as multi-billion dollar team sales and massive media rights push IPL's commercial engine into a dizzying overdrive.

Bizarre: Sai Sudharsan throws his bat onto the stumps, out hit-wicket in Qualifier 1Gujarat Titans opener Sai Sudharsan suffered one of the most bizarre dismissals in IPL history during the Qualifier 1 clash against Royal Challengers Bengaluru. Attempting to play a cut shot, Sudharsan accidentally flung his bat onto the stumps behind him, resulting in a freak hit-wicket dismissal.

Scorching Paris heat? Coco Gauff keeps it quick for fans at Roland GarrosFrench Open 2026: Coco Gauff beat Taylor Townsend 6-4, 6-0 to begin her French Open title defence in punishing Paris heat. After a nervy start, the defending champion found her calm, handled the conditions and moved into the second round.

England add Danni Wyatt-Hodge, Maia Bouchier for women's T20Is against IndiaEngland opener Danni Wyatt-Hodge will be available for the second and third T20Is against India following the birth of her first child. England and India will face off in the series beginning at Chelmsford on Thursday ahead of the women's T20 World Cup next month.

Qualifier 1: Virat Kohli's 10-year-wait for fifty in IPL playoffs continuesVirat Kohli's has not scored a fifty in the IPL playoffs since 2026. Kohli has played 17 IPL innings in his life, where he has scored 396 runs with a strike-rate of 121.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's maturity amazes Shanaka: Haven't seen a kid like thatVaibhav Sooryavanshi has taken the IPL by storm with his fearless batting, but it is his calmness and maturity off the field that has left Rajasthan Royals teammate Dasun Shanaka most impressed.

Afghanistan announce squads for India tour, Rashid Khan skips one-off TestAfghanistan announced their squads for the upcoming tour of India on Tuesday, confirming that star spinner Rashid Khan will skip the one-off Test match but return for the ODI series beginning later in June.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
